intro: Freziera lanata, also known as the woolly Freziera or woolly bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Freziera lanata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and caffeic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Freziera lanata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
